evidence.scientific_clarity: All brief requirements met: 12 studies with CI lines, squares sized by inverse-variance weight, red diamond for fixed-effects pooled estimate, dashed vertical line at LOR=0.
evidence.typography: Script sets no pdf.fonttype or svg.fonttype; PDF confirmed as /Subtype /Type3 (bitmap glyphs, non-selectable); svg.fonttype defaults to 'path'; font is DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default, not publication-quality).
evidence.palette: Study squares use #2166ac (ColorBrewer blue) and pooled diamond uses #b2182b (ColorBrewer red) — both from RdBu, colorblind-aware; red reserved for the special pooled marker rather than directional coding, which is acceptable but could imply 'bad' direction.
evidence.layout_density: 8x6 inch figure for 12 studies is reasonable; separator line between studies and pooled; right-side annotation for pooled estimate adds 0.35 xlim padding; no overlapping labels expected, but annotation text may be tight near the right edge.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure and includes specific numerical finding; no in-figure legend (symbols explained only in caption); figure title is redundant with caption and would typically be removed for journal submission.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf exists (18 KB) but PDF strings confirm /Subtype /Type3 DejaVuSans — text is rendered as bitmap glyphs, not selectable or editable; pdf.fonttype=42 was never set.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (18 KB), figure.png (51 KB), figure.svg (48 KB), caption.tex (507 B), gen_figure.py (2945 B).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the effect measure (LOR), explains symbol encoding (squares, CI lines, diamond), states the pooled result with exact values (LOR=0.245; 95% CI: 0.196–0.294), and identifies the method (inverse-variance fixed-effects weighting).

overall_strengths: Scientific implementation is correct and complete: inverse-variance weighting, proportional squares, diamond pooled estimate, and null-effect reference line all match the brief.
Caption is publication-ready: specific numerical claim, methodology named, all visual elements explained.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype='none') never set — PDF embeds Type 3 bitmap fonts, making text non-selectable and failing most journal vector-text requirements.
Default DejaVu Sans font and no in-figure legend; a journal reviewer would request a publication font (e.g. Helvetica/Arial) and an explicit legend or symbol key within the panel.
